task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the Junk folder to review the emails.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email client shows the contents of the Junk folder at some point in the trajectory.

task_completion.description: Verify a new folder named 'False-Positives' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The folder pane or sidebar of the email client displays a folder named 'False-Positives'.

task_completion.description: Verify emails were moved into the 'False-Positives' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'False-Positives' folder is selected/open and contains email messages.

task_completion.description: Verify exactly 5 emails are present in the 'False-Positives' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The message list for the 'False-Positives' folder shows exactly 5 emails, or a folder item count indicates 5 messages.

task_completion.description: Verify the draft email has the correct recipient and subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email compose window is open with 'helpdesk-admin@techcorp.org' in the 'To' field and 'Spam Filter Incident Report' in the 'Subject' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the draft email reports the correct number of rescued emails.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body of the drafted email explicitly states that 5 legitimate emails (or false positives) were rescued.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the email client's graphical interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with an email application's GUI (clicking folders, selecting emails, using menus) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the email is drafted in the email client's composition window.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draft is located in an actual email composition window with standard email UI elements (Send button, formatting toolbar), not a generic text editor like Notepad, Gedit, or Nano.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not cheat by moving files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown executing `mv`, `cp`, or similar commands on `.eml` files to bypass the email client.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target folder: 'False-Positives'. Report recipient: 'helpdesk-admin@techcorp.org'. There are exactly 5 legitimate emails (false positives) to be rescued from the Junk folder. The drafted email must report exactly 5 rescued emails.
